name: SystemSkillOperationContracts
description: Concrete request, result, compatibility, and stable-error contracts for schema discovery, version reporting, and bundled skill deployment.
schema_def: All operations use the common OperationDescriptor envelopes. skill.install is a mutation and requires --idempotency-key.
- result: {operations: [{operationId, cliPath, kind, minimumContractVersion, maximumContractVersion, summary}]}
- result: {descriptor: {operationId, cliPath, kind, contractVersion, requestSchema, resultSchema, errors, exits, examples}}
- result: {executableVersion, minimumContractVersion, maximumContractVersion, currentStorageVersion, supportedStorageVersions, bundledSkillManifestVersion}
- result: {skills: [{skillId, provider, version, compatibleExecutableRange, compatibleContractRange, checksum}]}
- result: {provider, scope, installedPath?, installedVersion?, bundledVersion, compatibility: compatible|missing|outdated|newer|invalid, reasons[]}
- result: {provider, scope, installedPath, installedVersion, checksum, compatibility}
- errors: TALLY-SKILL-PROVIDER-UNSUPPORTED exit7; TALLY-SKILL-INCOMPATIBLE exit7; TALLY-SKILL-PATH-UNSAFE exit3; TALLY-SKILL-PERMISSION-DENIED exit9; TALLY-SKILL-MANIFEST-INVALID exit8

Constraints: descriptors and manifests are generated from the executable-owned registry at build time; schema discovery requires no store. Install writes only the documented provider/scope location, verifies checksum and compatibility after publication, and returns the stored replay result on idempotent replay.
